alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has approximately eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, indicating post-degree professional experience well beyond undergraduate level.
alignments.job_requirement: Currently pursuing or recently completed a Bachelor's degree (ideally in Law, Business Administration, Criminology, Finance, or a related field).

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has professional/fluent English and native German language proficiency; Spanish not mentioned.
alignments.job_requirement: Professional fluency in English (written and spoken), German and Spanish a plus.

alignments.candidate_evidence: No evidence of AML/KYC expertise, financial crime prevention domain knowledge, or familiarity with Spanish regulatory frameworks provided in profile.
alignments.job_requirement: A strong interest in financial crime prevention, with basic academic or practical knowledge of the Spanish regulatory framework (Law 10/2010 and Sepblac guidelines) being highly advantageous.

gaps.gap: Internship-level scope: KYC Operations Intern role is designed for student or recent graduate entry-level work; candidate has eight years of senior operational experience at Google and two promotions.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate is significantly over-qualified; no mitigation bridges the fundamental misalignment of seniority and role ambition.

gaps.gap: Domain expertise: No AML/KYC/financial crime prevention background; no evidence of regulatory compliance, customer due diligence, or transaction monitoring domain knowledge.
gaps.mitigation: Strong operations and compliance fundamentals could accelerate learning, but candidate lacks vertical domain entry points and would require extensive ramp-up in specialized compliance regulatory frameworks.

gaps.gap: Spanish regulatory framework: Job explicitly highlights Spanish regulatory knowledge (Law 10/2010, Sepblac) as highly advantageous; candidate profile does not mention this.
gaps.mitigation: Self-study is theoretically possible but JD signals Madrid team and Spanish market focus; lack of pre-existing compliance domain knowledge compounds this gap.

output.level_rationale: KYC Operations Intern role explicitly targets student or recent-graduate entry level (0–2 years post-degree equivalent). JD describes day-to-day case reviews, document management, and administrative support under team-lead supervision. No management scope, no strategic ownership. Classic L3 internship profile.
output.summary: This is an L3 KYC Operations Intern role—entry-level, compliance-operations domain, student/recent-graduate target. Candidate is an L4–L5 Strategy and Operations leader with eight years at Google and no AML/KYC/financial-crime domain background. Seniority mismatch (internship vs. mid-career professional) and complete absence of compliance/regulatory vertical expertise make this a severe fit gap. Role family (compliance operations) sits far outside target families (Strategy, BizOps, Product Ops, GTM). Advisory: skip.
uncertainties: Whether candidate has undisclosed interest in career pivot into compliance/fintech but no evidence in profile.
N26 company size/stage context not stated in Role JSON; no mitigation to seniority gap if this were an atypical early-stage exception.
